steps.title: Apply angle-angle similarity criterion
steps.content: We already have angle L = angle R = 60°. For AA similarity, we need one more pair of equal angles. Choices A and B provide only side information, not angles. Focus on choices C and D, which give angle measures.

steps.title: Check choice C: angle M = 70° and angle S = 60°
steps.content:
In triangle LMN: angle L = 60°, angle M = 70°, so angle N = 50°.
In triangle RST: angle R = 60°, angle S = 60°, so angle T = 60°.
Triangle LMN has angles {60°, 70°, 50°}. Triangle RST has angles {60°, 60°, 60°}. The angle sets differ, so the triangles are not similar.

steps.title: Check choice D: angle M = 70° and angle T = 50°
steps.content:
In triangle LMN: angle L = 60°, angle M = 70°, so angle N = 50°.
In triangle RST: angle R = 60°, angle T = 50°, so angle S = 70°.
Triangle LMN has angles 60°, 70°, 50° at vertices L, M, N. Triangle RST has angles 60°, 70°, 50° at vertices R, S, T. All angles match under the correspondence L ↔ R, M ↔ S, N ↔ T. By AA similarity, the triangles are similar. That matches choice D — D is correct.
